name: StandardApi

unions.name: Direction
unions.comment: The `Direction` type is used to order a query.

unions.name: Include
unions.comment:
The `Include` type is for including related results in a query. For example,
in the query `{ emptyQuery | includes = [ include ( "line_items", emptyQuery ) ]`
would include related `line_items` in the response.

unions.name: Operation
unions.comment: The `Operation` type is used for making comparisons in a predicate.

unions.name: Value
unions.comment:
A `Value` is used for making comparisons. In the example `x > 3`, `3` is
the value.

aliases.name: Attribute
aliases.type: { name : String.String, type_ : StandardApi.Type.Type, default : Maybe.Maybe String.String, primaryKey : Basics.Bool, null : Basics.Bool, array : Basics.Bool, comment : String.String }

aliases.name: Config
aliases.type: { url : Url.Url, headers : List.List ( String.String, String.String ), timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, format : String.String, version : String.String }

aliases.name: Limit
aliases.comment:
The `Limit` type for limiting the results of a query. If the value is
`Nothing`, the default value is used (which may be unlimited). If the value is
a `Just x`, then the limit will be x.

aliases.name: Model
aliases.type: { name : String.String, attributes : List.List StandardApi.Attribute, comment : String.String }

aliases.name: Offset
aliases.comment:
The `Offset` type is for offseting the results of a query. If the value is
`Nothing`, there will be no offset (the same as `Just 0`). If the value is
a `Just x`, then the offset will be x.

aliases.name: Order
aliases.comment:
The `Order` type is used to order a query. It is represented as a list
containing a tuple with the name of the colum and the direction to order.

aliases.name: Query
aliases.comment:
A `Query` is used to make a request against the server. This is similar to a
SQL query.
aliases.type: { limit : StandardApi.Limit, order : StandardApi.Order, offset : StandardApi.Offset, predicate : Maybe.Maybe StandardApi.Operation, includes : List.List (Tree.Tree StandardApi.Include) }

aliases.name: Route
aliases.type: { path : String.String, method : String.String, model : Maybe.Maybe StandardApi.Model, array : Basics.Bool, limit : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, wheres : List.List StandardApi.Attribute, orders : List.List String.String, includes : List.List (Tree.Tree String.String) }

aliases.name: Schema
aliases.type: { comment : String.String, models : List.List StandardApi.Model, routes : List.List StandardApi.Route }

values.name: cancel
values.comment: Try to cancel an ongoing request based on a tracker.

values.name: emptyBody
values.comment:
An empty body for a request. This is simply an alias for
`Http.emptyBody` so you don't need to import `Http`.

values.name: emptyQuery
values.comment: An empty query.

values.name: errorToString
values.comment: Convert a `Error` into a `String` that is nice for debugging.

values.name: expectJson
values.comment:
Just like [`Http.expectJson`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/http/latest/Http#expectJson),
but for decoding a JSON value in StandardAPI.
values.type: (Result.Result StandardApi.Error a -> msg) -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> Http.Expect msg

values.name: expectWhatever
values.comment:
Just like [`Http.expectWhatever`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/http/latest/Http#expectWhatever),
but for decoding a JSON value in StandardAPI.

values.name: include
values.comment:
Helper method to generate an include.

    import StandardApi exposing (..)

    include ("users", { emptyQuery | limit = Just 1 })
    --> Include ("users", { emptyQuery | limit = Just 1 })

values.name: jsonBody
values.comment:
Put a JSON value in the body of the request. This is simply an alias for
`Http.jsonBody` so you don't need to import `Http`.

values.name: jsonResolver
values.comment:
Just like [`Http.stringResolver`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/http/latest/Http#stringResolver),
but for decoding a JSON value.

values.name: modelToTypes
values.comment:
Convert a `Model` to a list of `( String, Type )` pairs suitable for
passing to `StandardApi.Url.Parser.Query.parse`.

    import StandardApi.Type as Type

    modelToTypes { name = "test", attributes = [ { name = "id", type_ = Type.Int, default = Nothing, primaryKey = True, null = False, array = False, comment = "" } ], comment = "" }
    --> [ ( "id", Type.Int ) ]

values.name: request
values.comment:
Create a HTTP request to a StandardAPI enpoint.

    import Url exposing (Protocol(..))

    config =
        { url =
            { protocol = Https
            , host = "example.com"
            , port_ = Nothing
            , path = ""
            , query = Nothing
            , fragment = Nothing
            }
        , headers = [ ( "Api-Key", "token") ]
        , timeout = Just 20
        , format = "application/json"
        , version = "0.1.0"
        }

    request config
        { method = "GET"
        , headers = []
        , path = "/posts"
        , body = emptyBody
        , expect = expectJson ReceivedPosts (list postDecoder)
        , tracker = Nothing
        }
values.type: StandardApi.Config -> { method : String.String, headers : List.List Http.Header, path : String.String, body : Http.Body, expect : Http.Expect msg, tracker : Maybe.Maybe String.String } -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: requestTask
values.comment: Just like [`request`](#request), but it creates a `Task`.
values.type: StandardApi.Config -> { method : String.String, headers : List.List Http.Header, path : String.String, body : Http.Body, resolver : Http.Resolver StandardApi.Error a } -> Task.Task StandardApi.Error a

values.name: schemaRequest
values.comment: Request the `Schema` from a StandardAPI resource.
values.type: StandardApi.Config -> { msg : Result.Result StandardApi.Error StandardApi.Schema -> msg, tracker : Maybe.Maybe String.String } -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: schemaRequestTask
values.comment: Just like `schemaRequest`, but it creates a `Task`.

values.name: unwrapInclude
values.comment:
Helper method to unwrap and include into the name of the include and its
subinclude. This is necessary due to recursion in the types.

    import StandardApi exposing (..)

    unwrapInclude (include ("users", { emptyQuery | limit = Just 1 }))
    --> ("users", { emptyQuery | limit = Just 1 })

name: StandardApi.Type
comment:
Types for StandardAPI attributes.

@docs Type, fromString

unions.name: Type
unions.comment:
The type of a StandardAPI attribute. This mirrors the value types in
`StandardApi.Value` and is used by the schema decoder and URL query parser
to determine how to interpret raw string values.

values.name: fromString
values.comment:
Parse a type string from the server into a `Type`. Unrecognized types
default to `String` so that the attribute decoder never fails on unknown
PostgreSQL or Rails types.

    fromString "integer"
    --> Ok Int

    fromString "string"
    --> Ok String

    fromString "datetime"
    --> Ok Posix

    fromString "decimal"
    --> Ok Decimal

    fromString "boolean"
    --> Ok Bool

    fromString "hash"
    --> Ok Dict

    fromString "text"
    --> Ok String

    fromString "uuid"
    --> Ok String

    fromString "jsonb"
    --> Ok Dict

    fromString "numeric"
    --> Ok Float

    fromString "date"
    --> Ok String

    fromString "inet"
    --> Ok String

name: StandardApi.Url.Builder
comment:
## Generation

@docs absolute, includes, limit, order, predicate, query, offset

values.name: absolute
includes
values.type: List.List String.String -> List.List (Tree.Tree StandardApi.Include) -> List.List Url.Builder.QueryParameter

values.name: limit
offset
order
predicate
values.comment: Convert a `Operation` to a `QueryParameter` to send to a StandardAPI server.

values.name: query
values.comment: Convert a Query to a list of query parameters.

name: StandardApi.Url.Parser.Query
comment:
Parse URL query parameters back into a StandardAPI `Query`.

This is the inverse of `StandardApi.Url.Builder.query`.

The parser takes a list of `( String, Type )` pairs that map column names
to their types. For columns with a known type, the parser decodes values
accordingly (e.g., integers, booleans, timestamps). For unknown columns,
values are guessed from the raw string.

If a type is specified for a column and the raw string cannot be parsed as
that type, the parser returns an error.

@docs parse, parseUrl

values.name: parse
values.type: List.List ( String.String, StandardApi.Type.Type ) -> String.String -> Result.Result String.String StandardApi.Query

values.name: parseUrl
values.comment:
Parse a `Url` into a `Query` using the given type mappings.

This is a convenience wrapper around `parse` that extracts the query
string from a `Url`.
values.type: List.List ( String.String, StandardApi.Type.Type ) -> Url.Url -> Result.Result String.String StandardApi.Query
